title: dryopteris filix-mas subsp. brittonii
intro: Dryopteris filix-mas subsp. brittonii, also known as the Britton's male fern, is a subspecies of the male fern (Dryopteris filix-mas) that is native to North America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate digestion and alleviate symptoms associated with gastrointestinal issues. The Britton's male fern has been used to treat a range of health issues, including constipation, indigestion, and bloating, thanks to its high content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infusion by steeping the leaves in hot water, which can be used to support digestive health and relieve symptoms of digestive discomfort.
